endpoint.name: Overall survival for patients not receiving definitive surgery
endpoint.definition: 1, 5 year survival
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Better: male sex, having private insurance, and living 10–50 miles from their treatment facility were associated with improved OS. 

Worse: age ≥65 years, black race, living in a
census tract with a median income < $30,000, tumor size ≥5 cm, and not receiving definitive surgery

endpoint.name: Overall survival for patients receiving definitive survery
endpoint.definition: 1 and 5 year
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Better: male sex, having private insurance, and living within 10 miles of a patient’s treatment facility 

Worse: age ≥65 years, living in a census tract with a median income < $30,000, tumor size ≥5 cm, positive margins at surgery

and living within 10 miles of a patient’s treatment facility

Patients with histologically proven squamous cell carcinoma tissue examined by microscope rather than cytology alone;
data.exclusion_criteria: Patients who had received part or all of their treatment outside the NCDB reporting facility; patients whose treatment information was missing, for whom the sequence of treatments was unclear, or whose staging information was inconsistent with treatment information or could not be assessed

endpoint.name: Primary treatment modality
endpoint.definition:
Patients were categorized as receiving “primary radiation” or “primary surgical” treatment.
The primary treatment modality was determined to be radiation if they did not receive primary surgery but underwent radiation.
It was classified as surgery if they received surgery before any radiation or chemotherapy.
“Local tumor excision” (e.g., excisional biopsy) was not considered primary surgery.
For tonsil and other oropharyngeal tumors, “pharyngectomy” procedures were included (91% were “limited/partial pharyngectomy”).
For base of tongue tumors, only patients who underwent glossectomy or more extensive surgery were classified as surgical (not local excision).

endpoint.name: Adverse pathologic features
endpoint.definition: the presence of adverse pathologic features among those who underwent primary surgical treatment.  For this analysis, they included only those patients undergoing primary surgery who had complete information on margin status and ECS

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)

endpoint.significant_attribute:
Statistically significant variables on univarate analysis: age,ACE-27 comorbidity, smoking, site, T classification, N classification, stage group.

Better OS: Younger patients

Poor OS: The surrogate (Cumulative Illness Rating Scale) for heavier smoking (HR = 2.88; p<.0001)

No significant difference: CRT vs RT alone

Statistically significant variables on univarate analysis: age,ACE-27 comorbidity, smoking, site, T classification, N classification, stage group.

Poor OS: The surrogate (Cumulative Illness Rating Scale) for heavier smoking (HR = 2.72; p<.0001).

endpoint.significant_attribute: No significant difference between CRT and RT (p = 0.22)

data.cancer_type: Oral squamous cell carcinoma (anterior tongue, floor of mouth)
data.notes:
FIA: 
Good focus on surgical de-escalation trend in early-stage oral SCC; detailed temporal analysis
data.other_inclusion_criteria: Clinical staging T1 or T2; anterior tongue or floor of mouth primary site, between 1992 and 2007.
data.exclusion_criteria: None explicitly mentioned beyond stage/site restrictions

endpoint.name: Overall survival
endpoint.definition:
Measured over up to 5 years using Kaplan–Meier method; 
survival compared across 4 time periods.
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Extracapsular spread (ECS) (strongest predictor), Age, Margin status (independent predictors), listed by Multivariable Cox model.
Age, clinical T-stage, clinical N-stage, operation with or without radiotherapy, free-flap surgery, p-stage, positive nodes, and ECS were found to be predictors of poor survival.

endpoint.name: Local recurrence
endpoint.definition: First event of recurrence at primary site
endpoint.significant_attribute: Rates of local recurrence were reported for patients without ECS by time period, but no clear significant predictors isolated in multivariable model.

endpoint.name: Regional recurrence
endpoint.definition: First event of lymph node recurrence
endpoint.significant_attribute: Similar to local recurrence, rates of regional recurrence were reported for patients without ECS by time period, but no clear significant predictors isolated in multivariable model.

data_source.source_type: Regional Maxillofacial Oncology Database, University Hospital Aintree, Liverpool.

attribute.name: Sex
attribute.unit: if available, unit of category or measurement e.g. day, Gy

attribute.name: Age
attribute.unit: Years (for Age)
attribute.category:
Mean age across time periods ~58–62 years (Table 1) 
In the Cox regression model, Age was found to a continuous variable

attribute.name: Clinical N-stage
attribute.measurement:
19% N+ in 1992–1995
13% N+ in 1996–1999
21% N+ in 2000–2003
19% N+ in 2004–2007
Going by the formula N0 = 100% – N+,
patients with clinically N0 were 81%, 87%, 79%, and 81% respectively.

attribute.category: Clear, Close, Involved, Positive nodes, Extracapsular spread, P-stage 3/4

attribute.measurement: Not reported as specific frequencies in this article

endpoint.name: primary treatment
endpoint.definition: The primary treatment modality was determined to be radiation if they did not receive primary surgery but had undergone radiation. The primary modality was surgery if they received surgery before any radiation or chemotherapy. A “local tumor excision” such as excisional biopsy was not considered primary surgery.

data.cancer_type: Squamous cell carcinoma of oral cavity, oropharynx, hypopharynx, or larynx
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
Surgery with negative margins, 
no extracapsular extension (ECE), 
survival ≥6 months from diagnosis,
those who received chemotherapy and radiation therapy within 14 days of each other
data.exclusion_criteria:
Positive margins, 
extracapsular extension, 
those who received preoperative radiation (i.e., having an unknown sequence between radiation and surgery), 
those who were diagnosed with this as a second primary malignancy, those for whom radiation was recommended but not administered or radiation was contraindicated because of other risk factors.

endpoint.name: Overall Survival
endpoint.definition: Time from diagnosis to death from any cause (not stated in the paper)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
5-year overall survival: 68.8% for surgery alone, 74.0% for S + RT, 87.8% for S + CRT

Improved OS:
Surgery + CRT (HR = 0.57, 95% CI: 0.39–0.81, P = .002)
Oropharynx site (HR = 0.34, 95% CI = 0.24-0.47, P < .001)

Decreased OS:
Age ≥70 (HR = 1.52, 95% CI = 1.06-2.18, P = .02)
Comorbidity score 1 (HR = 1.48, 95% CI = 1.04-2.12, P = .03)
pT2 stage (HR = 1.65, 95% CI = 1.22-2.22, P < .001)

attribute.unit: Years

attribute.category: None, Private insurance, Medicaid, Medicare, Other/unknown

attribute.name: Treatment Type
attribute.category: Surgery alone, Surgery + RT (radiotherapy), Surgery + CRT (chemoradiotherapy)

data.stage: 0, I, II, III, IV (Combined clinical and pathologic staging)
data.other_inclusion_criteria: Diagnosed with laryngeal SCC between 1985 and 2001. Cases included patients with first primaries and subsequent primaries.
data.exclusion_criteria: Verrucous carcinoma

endpoint.name: Overall Survival
endpoint.definition: Time from diagnosis to death from any cause
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Poor Survival: Nonsurgical treatment is associated with T3N0M0 glottic SCC. 
Better Survival: The subset of nonsurgical patients treated with chemoradiation fared better than those treated with irradiation alone.

endpoint.name: Relative Survival
endpoint.definition:
Relative survival was calculated by dividing the observed survival
rate by the expected survival rate of persons of similar race,
age, and sex.
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Poor survival: Advanced stage, non-surgical treatment, subglottis site. 

Better survival: Early-stage, surgical treatment, glottis site

endpoint.name: Observed Survival
endpoint.definition:
Observed survival denotes the actual percentage of patients that are
still alive at a specified time interval after diagnosis of cancer.
endpoint.significant_attribute: Not very sure

data.other_inclusion_criteria: treated and or diagnosed at reporting facility, first primary and subsequent primaries, the topography codes for the larynx (C32) included the following subsites: glottis (C32.0), supraglottis (C32.1), subglottis (C32.2), laryngeal cartilage (C32.3), overlapping lesion of larynx (C32.8), and larynx not otherwise specific (NOS)(C32.9). Anterior surface of epiglottis (C10.1) was included as a supraglottic site. Squamous cell carcinoma included the ICD-O 3 histologic codes (M8050 – 8084).

data.cancer_type: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (oral cavity, oropharynx, nasopharynx, hypopharynx, larynx, unknown primary)
data.stage: All stages included, but most patients had advanced (Stage III/IV) disease - 69% Stage IV, 17% Stage III
data.notes:
FIA: 
National-level registry; study focuses on real-world treatment and supportive care practices for HNC in academic vs community settings.
Sample size calculation: 4243 patients enrolled. 175 patients had salivary gland tumors and parotid bed metastases. These are not the target cancers (oral cavity, oropharynx, etc.), so excluded from analysis. 1 patient was excluded due to data quality issues. Thus, total excluded = 175 + 1 = 176 patients. However, further in the article, they mentioned that the electronic data forms (baseline assessment, initial treatment, follow-up, retreatment) had to be locked (i.e., reviewed, digitally signed, and passed internal checks). Therefore, only 3599 patients (Refer to table 1) had fully completed, error-free data available and were locked and complete for final descriptive analysis.


3599 included in main analyses.
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
1. Aged ≥18 years
2. Newly diagnosed, histologically confirmed carcinoma
3. Must be scheduled to receive treatment other than surgery alone
data.exclusion_criteria: Surgery alone as the only planned treatment

endpoint.name: Overall survival
endpoint.definition:
Median follow-up 1.1 years; maximum follow up 4.3 years; 
1-, 2-, and 3-year survival rates reported (86%, 76%, 69%)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Not clearly stated. Differences in treatment patterns by setting (academic vs community) and baseline factors like stage, age likely influence survival but not directly analyzed.

More explanation: They did not perform a multivariable analysis (like Cox regression) directly linking survival to specific factors like age, stage, treatment type, etc. Instead, most of their statistical analyses about significant attributes were related to which patients were more likely to be treated at academic vs community settings (Table 3, logistic regression) Or What kinds of treatments they received based on patient characteristics (e.g., the patients are more younger in the academic settings, and received adjuvant CRT/BRT, IMRT, single-agent cisplatin, TPF/cetuximab, tracheotomy, opioid analgesics, and antiemetics.).

endpoint.name: Locoregional control
endpoint.definition: 1-, 2-, and 3-year rates of locoregional control were 95%, 93%, and 92%,respectively.
endpoint.significant_attribute: Not attributed to specific predictors in the text

Age: P-value < 0.01

Insurance - Not insured: P-value = 0.02

Primary site Palate: P-value < 0.01
Overall Pathological Stage-III P-value < 0.01
Overall Pathological Stage-IVA/IVB P-value < 0.01
Surgical margins Positive P-value < 0.01

endpoint.name: Receipt of Adjuvant Chemotherapy
endpoint.definition: Whether patients received postoperative Chemotherapy
endpoint.significant_attribute:
OR (95% CI):
Pathologic Stage III	3.19 (1.42–7.21) More likely to receive chemo
Pathologic Stage IVA/IVB 7.66 (3.59–16.37)	Much more likely
Positive Surgical Margins 1.70 (1.14–2.54) More likely
Received Adjuvant Radiation	17.81 (4.34–73.04)	Extremely more likely
Urban Residence (vs rural/metro)	0.18 (0.06–0.54) Less likely to receive chemo

endpoint.name: Clinical Nodal Status Relationship to Pathological Nodal Status
endpoint.definition: Comparison of pre-operative (clinical) nodal status with post-operative (pathologic) nodal findings in patients who underwent neck dissection
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Clinically node-positive patients (n=194): 139/170 (81.8%) were also pathologically node-positive
Clinically node-negative patients (n=2,059): 98/1,312 (7.5%) had occult nodal metastases
24 clinically node-positive patients did not undergo neck dissection (reason unknown)
36.3% of clinically node-negative patients did not undergo a neck dissection

endpoint.name: Predictors of Positive Surgical Margins
endpoint.definition: Proportion of patients with microscopically positive margins following definitive resection for ACC, and the predictors associated with this outcome
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Stage II vs I: OR = 1.76 (95% CI: 1.33–2.31)
Stage III vs I: OR = 2.96 (95% CI: 2.22–3.93)
Stage IVA/IVB vs I: OR = 3.57 (95% CI: 2.72–4.70)
Delay of 3 months from diagnosis to surgery: OR = 1.36 (95% CI: 1.09–1.68)

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.definition: Time from surgery to death from any cause; evaluated using unadjusted Kaplan-Meier analysis, multivariable Cox proportional hazards modeling, and propensity-score matching
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Worse OS associated with: 
- Older age (HR = 1.03, 95% CI: 1.02–1.04) 
- Male (HR = 1.27, 95% CI: 1.03–1.56) 
- Advanced pathological T and N stage (exact HRs not provided) 
- Positive surgical margins (HR = 1.24, 95% CI: 1.01–1.53) 
- Treatment in the South (HR = 1.48, 95% CI)

Better OS associated with: 
- Private insurance (HR = 0.67, 95% CI: 0.51–0.89) 
- Charlson-Deyo comorbidity score of 0 (HR = 0.72, 95% CI: 0.56–0.94) 

Adjuvant radiation:
- Not significantly associated with OS in multivariable model (p = 0.57) - Not associated with OS in propensity-score matched cohort (HR = 0.90, 95% CI: 0.65–1.23)

endpoint.name: Cause-Specific Survival (CSS)
endpoint.definition: CSS was evaluated from the date of diagnosis to the most recent follow-up date and was calculated using the Kaplan-Meier method
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Age is independent predictor in both uni- and multi-variate analysis (95% CI).
After a median follow-up of 2.5 years, the 2-year cause-specific survival rate after definitive RT was 72% (range, 65–78%) for the elderly vs. 86% (range, 84–88%) for the younger patients (p <.01).

endpoint.name: Treatment Tolerance
endpoint.definition:
Ability to complete planned RT without unplanned interruption, incomplete RT, or treatment-related death
(Not exactly stated like this in the paper, but I deduced this definition after reading)
endpoint.significant_attribute: No significant difference between elderly (≥75 years) and younger patients (<75 years) in terms of Unplanned RT interruptions, Incomplete RT, Treatment-related deaths. Overall tolerance similar, suggesting age alone should not exclude patients from curative RT.

low performance status (HR=2.584; 95% CI 1.561–4.274; p<0.001), smoking history (HR=1.960; 95% CI 1.109–3.464; p<0.05) - found in cox multivariate analysis.

Improved OS: Younger age (65–74), (p<0.05)

endpoint.name: Cause-Specific Survival (CSS)
endpoint.definition:
Our main outcome, death from laryngeal cancer, was determined from the cause of death information in the Ontario Cancer Registry, which was obtained from a death certificate clearance using information from the Ontario Registrar General. There were no unknown causes of death. Death 
 was attributed to the larynx cancer if the cause of death was laryngeal cancer or if it was another head and neck cancer in the absence of a second primary to that region. Vital status was determined as of the date of the
chart abstraction and those still alive were censored on that date. The median follow-up was 50 months (interquartile range 22e96 months).
The Kaplane Meier method was used to generate cause-specific survival curves by cancer centre and the Log-rank test was used to assess statistical significance for overall, cause-specific and other-cause survival differences across centres.

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)

endpoint.significant_attribute:
Univariable Cox regression:

Worse OS:
subglottis (P-value =0.02) and larynx (no subsite specified) primary subsites (P-value =0.04), locally advanced (P-value =0.05), metastatic disease (P-value < 0.001)

Improved OS:
Radiation dose ≥40 Gy (P < .001)

Multi-variate Cox regression:
Worse OS:
locally advanced disease (P-value =0.02), metastatic disease (P-value < 0.001), and radiation dose <40 Gy (P-value < 0.001)

Chemotherapy was less likely given in early stage disease (P = .001), Definitive radiation was less likely given in metastatic disease (P < .001).

data.notes:
GN
(For cost and healthcare utilization analysis, patients in the HNC cohorts were matched 1:1 to cancer-free control patients based on geographic region, gender, and age group)

1-They did their analysis on two groups: mHNC (1,042 people) and rHNC (324 people). Not sure how to show the sample size?
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
metastatic cohort:
-Patients were required to be free from any other malignancy prior to the initial HNC diagnosis date in the database.
-Patients were also required to have continuous
health plan eligibility for 6 months before and 6 months
after the index date
-Patients were required to to be 18 years or older as of the
index date.

recurrent cohort:
-Patients with recurrent, locally-advanced HNC had at least one HNC claim
-Patients were also required to be free from any other malignancy prior to the initial HNC diagnosis date in the database.
-In addition, patients had at least two HNC claims that occurred at least 180 days apart
-Eligible patients also received at least one HNC-related treatment (including chemotherapy, targeted therapy or radiation therapy) within a 60-day window around the index date.
-Patients were required to to be 18 years or older as of the
index date.
data.exclusion_criteria:
recurrent cohort:
-Patients could not have a claim for metastatic cancer (i.e., secondary tumor ICD-9-CM codes 197.xx and 198.xx) for the period between the initial HNC diagnosis date and 6 months following the index date.

data.cancer_type: Squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa
data.other_inclusion_criteria: Patients with SCC of the buccal mucosa who received planned definitive treatment/curative intent at Tri-Service General Hospital, M0 stage.
data.exclusion_criteria:
Non-SCC histology including verrucous carcinomas, adenoid cystic carcinoma, epimyoepithelial carcinoma, one mucoepidermoid carcinoma, and adenocarcinoma, were excluded.

Patients with synchronous multiple cancers of the oral cavity, a history of other cancers, and prior treatment elsewhere were excluded. 

Patients with distant metastases at presentation were also excluded

endpoint.name: Locoregional Control (LRC)
endpoint.definition:
5-year rate of disease-free control at the primary site and regional lymph nodes
(Not particularly stated in the paper)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Better LRC:
Surgery alone (T1–2N0 stage): 60.7%
Surgery + RT (T1–2N0 stage): 52.2%
Radiation dose >70Gy in RT alone group

Worse LRC: 
T3+T4 (p-value = .0006), 
N+ (p-value = .0029), 
RT alone, 
deeper invasion (muscle + bone) (p-value = .0003), 
skin invasion (p-value = .0003),

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.definition:
5-year overall survival from diagnosis
(not stated in the paper)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Univariate -
Better OS:
Early stage,
S+RT group (P-value <.0001),
Grade 1,
Invasion depth (Mucosa+submucosa),
No bone invasion,
No skin invasion

Worse OS:
Advanced stage (P-value = .0001),
T3+T4 stage (P-value = .0001),
N+ stage (P-value <.0001),
S+RT group (P-value <.0001),
Grade 2+3 (P-value = 0.0129),
Invasion depth (Muscle + Bone) (P-value <.0001),
Bone invasion (P-value = .0005),
Skin invasion (P-value <.0001)

Multi-variate=
N classification (P-value = .002), 
Invasion depth (P-value = .015), 
Treatment modality (P-value = .004)

endpoint.name: Cause-Specific Survival (CSS)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Better CSS:
Early stage,
S+RT group (P-value <.0001),
Grade 1 ,
Invasion depth (Mucosa+submucosa),
No bone invasion,
No skin invasion

Worse OS:
Advanced stage (P-value < .0001),
T3+T4 stage (P-value < .0001),
N+ stage (P-value <.0001),
S+RT group  (P-value <.0001),
Grade 2+3  (P-value = 0.0104),
Invasion depth (Muscle + Bone) (P-value <.0001),
Bone invasion  (P-value = .0003),
Skin invasion (P-value <.0001)

Multi-variate:
N classification (P-value = .006), 
Invasion depth (P-value = .008), 
Treatment modality (P-value = .014)

data.other_inclusion_criteria: - adult patients
data.exclusion_criteria:
- Patients with tumors positive for low-risk HPV types only
were excluded
- Patients with incomplete treatment
information or those who were treated by surgery and
chemotherapy or chemotherapy alone were omitted
- Patients
with unknown outcomes, last follow-up within 6 months of
diagnosis, and prior malignancies were excluded from the
survival analysis.

patients diagnosed with SCC of the oral tongue, floor of mouth (FOM), or lip,
Non-surgical management with definitive External Beam Radiotherapy (EBRT) with or without Brachytherapy (BT)

patients younger than 18 years of age, 
had metastatic disease,
underwent definitive surgery,
did not receive radiotherapy or received unknown therapy,
without followup data and those who were coded as deceased at time = 0

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.definition:
Time from diagnosis to death from any cause
(not specified in the paper)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Better OS: Receipt of BT (multivariate HR = 0.71, p<0.001)
Tumour location associated with better OS: lip (HR, 0.52; p< 0.001), FOM (HR, 0.87, p=0.017), alveolar ridge (HR, 0.81; p=0.012), 
buccal mucosa(HR,0.80, p=0.015)

Worse OS associated factors: age between 55-65 years (HR, 1.40; p<0.001), age ≥65 (HR = 2.13, p<0.001), black race(HR,1.29; p=0.055), larger tumors, regional disease (HR,1.22; p=0.001)

data.other_inclusion_criteria:
cases incident in 1997, 2004, and 2009,
cases with known treatment,
who received primary therapy (surgery alone or surgery and RT) or RTC (RT only (with or without chemotherapy)).
data.exclusion_criteria:
Unknown treatment status,
who received either no cancer-directed therapy or chemotherapy only were excluded from the bivariate analysis and multivariable models,

endpoint.name: Likelihood of receiving surgery vs RTC
endpoint.definition:
The likelihood of a patient receiving primary surgery versus radiotherapy/ chemoradiotherapy (RTC) based on several patient and tumor-level characteristics.
(Not explicitly defined but derived from Table 3)

Analysis Type: Multivariable logistic regression
Reported Metric: Odds Ratios (OR)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Significant factors:

All Stages:
Year of diagnosis = 2004: HR =  0.29(95% CI: 0.15 -0.55)
Year of diagnosis = 2009: HR = 0.17(95% CI: 0.09 - 0.33)
Stage group (AJCC 6th Ed.) IVA & IVB: HR = 0.36 (95% CI: 0.18 - 0.70) 
Stage group (AJCC 6th Ed.) IVC: HR = 0.28(95% CI: 0.08 - 0.97) 
Stage group (AJCC 6th Ed.) Unknown: HR = 0.25(95% CI: 0.12 - 0.52)

T4 stage only:
Year of diagnosis = 2004: HR = 0.19(95% CI: 0.07 - 0.52) 
Year of diagnosis = 2009: HR = 0.15(95% CI: 0.04 - 0.51) 
Stage group (AJCC 6th Ed.) Unknown: HR = 0.36(95% CI: 0.13 - 0.95) 
Hospital beds >= 300: HR = 3.33(95% CI: 1.67 - 6.66)

Higher odds of receiving primary surgery vs RTC

endpoint.name: Hazard of death
endpoint.definition:
How treatment impacts survival.
(surgery vs RTC)

Analysis Type: Cox proportional hazards model (survival analysis)
Reported Metric: Hazard Ratios (HR)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Significant factors:

All Stages:
Year of diagnosis = 2009: HR = 0.51 (95% CI: 0.33–0.78)
Year of diagnosis (continuous): HR = 1.03 (95% CI: 1.02–1.05)
Marital Status Unmarried: HR = 2.10 (95% CI: 1.51 - 2.91)
Charlson score >= 1: HR = 1.77(95% CI: 1.20 - 2.62)
Stage group (AJCC 6th Ed.) IVC: HR = 2.22(95% CI: 1.16 - 4.26)

T4 stage only:
Stage group (AJCC 6th Ed.) IVC: HR = 5.64 (95% CI: 3.00 - 10.58)

Primary surgery was associated with better overall survival than RTC

data.cancer_type: Squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
- patients <= 70 years old 
- underwent definitive surgery
- between 2004 and 2012
- staged as pT3-4N03M0 and/or pT1-4N0-3M0 with positive margins and/or ECE.
- received postoperative RT alone or postoperative CRT
data.exclusion_criteria:
- Patients who survived <6 months from diagnosis 
- who received neoadjuvant chemotherapy 
- who received surgery alone

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.definition: if available, definition of this endpoint
endpoint.significant_attribute:
5-year OS: 61.6% for postoperative RT and 67.4% for postoperative CRT (P < .001)

Multivariable analysis:
Improved OS: (1) Postoperative CRT (HR 0.87; 95% CI 0.82-0.93; P<.001), (2) Location of the primary tumour in a subsite other than the oral cavity (HR 0.31-0.72)

Worse OS: (1) pT3-4 disease (HR 2.00; 95% CI 1.85-2.15; P<.001), (2) N2 to N3 disease (HR 1.47; 95% CI 1.30-1.67; P<.001), (3) positive margins (HR 1.28; 95% CI 1.19-1.39; P<.001), (4) ECE (HR 1.40; 95% CI 1.31-1.49; P<.001)

Subset analysis:
5-year OS (negative margin, no ECE): 63.4% for postoperative RT and 69.7% for postoperative CRT (P < .001)
5-year OS (positive margins and/or ECE): 60.0% for postoperative RT and 66.2% for postoperative CRT (P < .001)

Multivariable Analysis with propensity-unmatched study: 
Improved OS: Postoperative CRT (HR 0.87; 95% CI 0.79-0.96; P5.006) and tumour location other than oral cavity (HR 0.29 for oropharynx, 0.67 for hypopharynx, and 0.62 for larynx)

Multivariable Analysis with propensity-matched study: 
Improved OS: treatment at an academic facility (HR 0.88; 95% CI 0.790.97; P5.01)
Worse OS: N2 to N3 disease (HR 1.52), positive margins (HR 1.27), ECE (HR 1.43).

data.other_inclusion_criteria: Plans submitted for sequential treatments (ie, concurrent chemoradiotherapy [CRT] after induction chemotherapy) for initial curative treatment of newly diagnosed patients with nonmetastatic cancers of the oral cavity, oropharynx, larynx, and hypopharynx were included.

data.exclusion_criteria:
- HNC patients who were not referred were excluded.
- First visit not between Feb-May of 2018, 2019, 2020
- Patients who received all definitive treatment at outside facilities, died, or transitioned to palliative care before treatment initiation were excluded.
- Duplicate entries were excluded
- Those for whom the 90-day Telehealth data were not collected were excluded

p < 0.001 for the increase in telehealth usage from 2018 to 2020.

endpoint.name: Time from Oncology Visit to Treatment

endpoint.significant_attribute:
2018: 23 days
2019: 24 days
2020: 32 days
P = 0.019
Time to initiation of treatment was only modestly delayed in 2020 compared to prior years.

valid assessment.

-Excluded patients with unknown timing of surgery after diagnosis.

-Excluded patients with in situ disease, incomplete pathologic staging, or those who received neoadjuvant radiotherapy or chemotherapy before surgery.

-Limited the cohort to patients who survived or had follow-up for ≥3 months after surgery.

data.other_inclusion_criteria:
Patients diagnosed with 
- mucosal melanoma of all non-cutaneous, mucosal head and neck sites between 2004 and 2012.
- localised disease with documented follow-up
- underwent definitive treatment
- complete clinical stage data
data.exclusion_criteria:
- Patients with non-mucosal lip cancers and salivary primaries 
- Unknown follow-up or vital status
- distant metastases
- undocumented receipt of chemotherapy
- unknown time of radiation with respect to surgery
- No definitive local therapy
- Non-exclusion surgical procedure
- received non-external beam radiation
- received inraoperative radiotherapy
- missing clinical tumour or nodal stating

endpoint.name: Overall Survival
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Surgery alone, radiotherapy alone, or combined surgery and radiotherapy, 5-year overall survival was 31.0%, 8.1%, and 26.9%(overall P<0.001) respectively.

Radiotherapy alone was also associated with decreased survival in comparison to patients undergoing surgery in both univariate analysis (hazard ratio [HR]=2.49, 95% [CI] =1.96–3.17, P<0.001) and multivariate analysis (HR=2.27, 95% CI=1.72–2.98, P<0.001)

Univariate Cox regression analysis of overall survival:
worse overall survival:
paranasal sinusmucosal melanoma (HR=1.61, 95% CI=1.37–1.89, P<0.001)

better overall survival:
Non-sinonasal tumor location (HR=0.75,95%CI=0.63–0.89,P<0.001)

Multivariate Cox regression analysis of overall survival:
worse overall survival:
paranasal sinusmucosal melanoma (HR=1.54, 95%CI=1.30–1.82, P<0.001)
age(HR=1.02 per year,95% CI=1.02–1.03, P<0.001),
clinical tumor stage cT4b (HR=2.09, 95%CI=1.34–3.25, P<0.001), Charlson comorbidity score of 2 or more (HR=1.58, 95% CI=1.06–2.33), 
clinical nodal stage cN1 (HR=2.22, 95% CI=1.46–3.38, P<0.001),
cNx(HR=1.98, 95%CI=1.24–3.15, P=0.004)

better overall survival:
Non-sinonasal tumor location (HR=0.71, 95%CI=0.58–0.86, P<0.001)
Sex (Female) (HR=0.82, 95% CI=0.72–0.94, P=0.005)

Multivariate analysis-
Better OS: 
Delivered radiation dose (HR: 0.97, 95% CI 0.96–0.99, p <.01), 
histologic sub type other than SCC (HR: 0.52, 95% CI 0.28–0.96, p <.01), 
a positive treatment response (HR: 0.39, 95% CI 0.21–0.73, p <.01)

Diagnosed with HPV-associated oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (OP-SCC)

IRFs defined using AJCC 7th edition TNM staging criteria

If received PORT or POCRT: Total radiation dose information must be available

Minimum radiation dose of 50 Gy for those receiving RT

data.cancer_type: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC)
data.notes:
GN 
- For sample size: 168,257*0.47 = 79,081
- The study doesn’t report clinical outcomes like survival or recurrence, it focuses on treatment receipt (CRT vs. RT, surgery vs. non-surgery) as the main outcomes to understand care patterns in older HNSCC patients.
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
-It was their first and only cancer diagnosis
-Aged 66 years or older
-Had at least 1 year of Medicare enrollment before diagnosis
-To assure data stability for examination of racial disparities, only those SEER registries with 5% or greater non-Caucasians were examined(13), including Greater California, Detroit, Hawaii, New Jersey and Connecticut, Louisiana, and Georgia.
data.exclusion_criteria:
Patients were excluded if they were diagnosed at autopsy or on death certificate,
had metastatic disease at diagnosis, had incomplete claims or less than one year of
continuous claims, or were missing information on date of diagnosis, qualifying treatment
codes, or other covariates.

data.other_inclusion_criteria:
- patients diagnosed with pathologic stage III to IVB OPSCC
- Diagnosed between 2010 and 2012
- HPV status was available
- The cancer must be the first or only malignancy
- Availability of data for 1) Date of surgery 2) Radiotherapy (RT) details 3) Chemotherapy information 4) RT dose details
- Must have undergone initial surgery to the primary tumour and neck
- Must have received postoperative radiotherapy (PORT) within 10 weeks of surgery.
- Surgery must include resection of the primary tumor (exclusing excisional biopsy)
- Must include neck surgery 
- Concurrent chemotherapy must have started between 1 week before the first fraction of RT up to 3 weeks after the first fraction
- Must have received a total RT dose between 59.4 Gy and 66.6 Gy, indicating radical adjuvant intent.
data.exclusion_criteria:
- Patients without complete treatment data (e.g., missing surgery, RT, chemo, or dose information)
- Patients whose cancer was not the first or only cancer
- Patients who did not undergo resection of the primary tumor
-  Patient who had only lymph node aspiration or neck sampling
- Patients who received PORT after 10 weeks post-surgery
- Patients who received radiation doses outside the 59.4–66.6 Gy range
- Patients whose chemotherapy was not concurrent as defined (outside −1 to +3 weeks of RT start)

endpoint.name: Treatment with CRT (Concurrent Chemoradiotherapy)
endpoint.definition: Receiving chemotherapy concurrently with postoperative radiotherapy (PORT)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Statistically Significant Predictors (from multivariable regression):

- HPV-positive cancer, OR 0.47, 95% CI 0.33–0.68 – Less likely to receive CRT compared to HPV-negative patients

- Positive surgical margins (PSM), OR 2.07, 95% CI 1.50–2.87 – More likely to receive CRT

- Extracapsular extension (ECE), OR 6.99, 95% CI 5.22–9.35 – More likely to receive CRT

Low neck disease, OR 1.52, 95% CI 1.01–2.28 – More likely to receive CRT

≥6 positive lymph nodes, OR 2.34, 95% CI 1.39–3.92 – More likely to receive CRT

Comprehensive community cancer center (vs academic institution), OR 1.59, 95% CI 1.21–2.10 – More likely to receive CRT at non-academic centers

Interaction term (Academic institution and No ECE/PSM), OR 0.43, 95% CI 0.31–0.60, P < 0.001 – Patients without high-risk features were significantly less likely to receive CRT at academic centers

endpoint.name: Delivery of Boost RT Dose >60 Gy
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Significant Predictors:

Positive surgical margins (PSM) – Significantly associated with receiving boost RT

Extracapsular extension (ECE) – Significantly associated with receiving boost RT

T4 tumor status – Significantly associated with receiving boost RT

HPV-positive cancer, OR 0.80, 95% CI 0.69–0.94 – Less likely to receive boost RT compared to HPV-negative

Northeast U.S. treatment location – Patients treated in the Northeast were approximately twice as likely to receive boost RT compared to those treated in the Midwest and West

Use of intensity-modulated radiotherapy (IMRT) – Less common in patients receiving boost RT

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
2-Year Overall Survival (OS) Rates
Entire cohort: 92% (95% CI, 90%–94%)
HPV-positive: 95% (93%–98%)
HPV-negative: 85% (79%–91%)

By treatment modality and risk group:
HPV-positive + no high-risk features -
PORT only: 100% (n=86)
CRT: 98% (94%–100%, n=54)

HPV-positive + high-risk features -
PORT only: 89% (76%–100%, n=41)
CRT: 93% (88%–97%, n=134)

Multivariable Predictors of OS:
HPV-positive disease → better OS - HR: 0.41 (95% CI, 0.21–0.80)
Positive surgical margin (PSM) → Worse OS
Higher T stage (T4) → Worse OS
≥6 positive lymph nodes → Worse OS
Older age, female sex, and lower education → Worse OS

data.cancer_type: Squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
- patients with a first oncologic diagnosis of larynx cancer 
- between 2004–2012 
- aged ≥18 years
data.exclusion_criteria:
- patients with cancer from sites other than the glottis 
- clinical stage other than TisN0M0, T1N0M0, T1aN0M0, T1bN0M0, or T2N0M0 
- patients receiving surgery or palliative interventions
-  those not receiving EBRT
- patients with missing survival information or incomplete information regarding their regional radiation dose, boost radiation dose, and number of fractions
-  patients with dose-per-fraction <150 cGy or >400 cGy, total RT dose >9,000 cGy, or >50 fractions 
- those with non-SCC histology codes 
- those with 2 months of follow-up

endpoint.name: Receipt of Hypofractionated Radiation Therapy (HFxn)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Multivariate Significant Predictors:

- Diagnosis year:
2007–2009: OR 1.55 (95% CI: 1.39–1.73), p<0.001 → More likely to receive HFxn
2010–2012: OR 2.68 (95% CI: 2.42–3.00), p<0.001 → More likely to receive HFxn

- Income:
Higher-income areas → Significantly associated with increased HFxn use
3rd quartile: OR 1.30 (95% CI: 1.15 – 1.48), p<0.001
4th quartile: OR 1.25 (95% CI: 1.10 – 1.43), p<0.001

- Facility type:
Comprehensive cancer programs: OR 1.43 (95% CI: 1.25–1.64), p<0.001
Academic/research facilities: OR 1.95 (95% CI: 1.69–2.25), p<0.001
→Patients at these facilities were more likely to receive HFxn

- Tumor stage
T2: OR 0.66 (95% CI: 0.55–0.80), p<0.001 → Less likely to receive HFxn compared to Tis

endpoint.name: Overall survival (OS)
endpoint.definition: time from diagnosis to death
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Multivariable Significant Predictors:

Worse OS associated with:
- Older age: 
61-70 - HR 1.35 (95% CI: 1.20–1.52), p<0.001
>70 - HR 2.29 (95% CI: 2.02–2.59), p<0.001

- Comorbidity score:
1 - HR 1.27 (95% CI: 1.15–1.40), p<0.001
2+ - HR 1.96 (95% CI: 1.68–2.28), p<0.001

- Insurance:
Medicaid: HR 1.53 (95% CI: 1.16 - 2.02), p=0.003

Improved OS associated with:
- Female gender: HR 1.27 (95% CI: 0.71–0.90), p<0.001
- Private insurance: HR 0.77 (95% CI: 0.61–0.99), p=0.039
- Higher income
3rd quartile: OR 0.84 (95% CI: 0.75 – 0.94), p=0.002
4th quartile: OR 0.79 (95% CI: 0.70 – 0.88), p<0.001

-Treatment at academic/research facility
OR 0.85 (95% CI: 0.75 – 0.96), p=0.008

- Tumor stage:
T1 vs Tis: HR 1.50 (95% CI: 1.24–1.82), p<0.001 → Worse OS
T2 vs Tis: HR 2.42 (95% CI: 1.99–2.94), p<0.001 → Worse OS

Fractionation regimen:
HFxn vs CFxn: HR 0.90 (95% CI: 0.83–0.97), p=0.008 → HFxn associated with improved OS

data.other_inclusion_criteria:
Patients with 
- larynx cancer 
- diagnosed from 2004-2014 
- aged 18years
- a Conventional fractionation (CFxn) group receiving 1.50–2.10 Gy per fraction at 4–6 fractions per week
- a HypoFxn group receiving 2.11–3.50 Gy per fraction at 4–6 fractions per week,
- a HyperFxn group receiving 1.00–2.50 Gy per fraction at 8–12 fractions per week
data.exclusion_criteria:
Patients with
- clinical stage other than T2N0M0
- cancer arising from a non-glottic subsite
- histologies other than squamous cell carcinoma
- a prior malignant diagnosis
- coded as receiving surgery
- not receiving EBRT
- receiving palliative interventions
- incomplete information regarding their EBRT dose, number of fractions, or EBRT duration
- whose dose-per-fraction or fraction frequency were not consistent with any of the three schedules defined in the inclusion criteria were excluded (coded as receiving <50 Gy or >90Gy).

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.definition: time from diagnosis to death
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Multivariable Cox Regression Analysis (MVA) – Predictors of Worse OS
Older Age (v <=60): (HR 2.09, 95%CI 1.72–2.56, P <0.001 ) 
Male Gender: (HR 0.79, 95%CI 0.65–0.95, P=0.014) 
Comorbidity Score > 0 (Vs 0): (HR 1.25, 95%CI 1.09–1.45, P=0.002) 
Medicaid (vs. Private)
(HR 1.84, 95%CI 1.22–2.77, P=0.004) 
Other Insurance (vs. Private)
(HR 1.89, 95%CI 1.17–3.03, P=0.009) 
Receiving care at an academic facility (vs non-academic)
(HR 0.86, 95%CI 0.73–1.00, P=0.047) 

Predictors of Better OS:
HypoFxn (Vs CFxn): (HR 0.84, 95%CI 0.73–0.97, p=0.017)
HyperFxn (Vs CFxn): (HR 0.74, 95%CI 0.56–0.99, p=0.044)

Propensity Score Matched (PSM) Analyses:
1. CFxn vs HypoFxn (HR: 0.82, 95% CI: 0.69 – 0.97, P = 0.019)
HypoFxn associated with improved OS

2. CFxn vs HyperFxn (HR: 0.56, 95% CI: 0.38 – 0.82, P = 0.003)
HyperFxn associated with improved OS

data.stage:
Clinical State I, II, III, IV
Pathological State I, II, III, IV
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
Patients who were:
- diagnosed with carcinosarcoma of the salivary gland between 2004 and 2018.
- Identified using ICD-O-3.1 code 8980/3 (carcinosarcoma, not otherwise specified).
- Primary tumor site in one of the major salivary glands, identified by site codes: C07.9, C08.0, C08.1, C08.8, C08.9
- Had surgical treatment (defined as a definitive intervention, not just biopsy).
- Microscopically negative margins after surgery were counted as negative margins.
data.exclusion_criteria:
Patients were excluded if they had:
- T0 tumors.
- Prior malignancies.
- Palliative treatment.
- Unknown outcomes.
- Less than 6 months of follow-up (for survival analysis).
- Non-surgical treatment

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Significant Predictors of Worse OS (Univariable Analysis):
- Overall Stage III-IV: (HR 2.50, 95% CI: 1.26-4.93, P = .008)
- Stage pT3 or pT4: (HR 2.74, 95% CI: 1.42-5.27, P = .003)
nodal metastases (pN+): (HR 1.73, 95% CI: 0.92-3.27, P = .09)
positive surgical margins: (HR 2.01, 95% CI: 1.15-3.51, P = .01)

Significant Predictors of Worse Overall  (Multivariable Analysis)

- age (HR, 1.02; 95%CI, 1.01-1.04; P=.03)
- pT3/pT4 disease (HR 2.51; 95%CI, 1.27-4.95; P= .008)

endpoint.name: response to treatment
endpoint.definition: Patients were considered to have a “complete” response if full locoregional control was achieved. Patients who did not respond to treatment, experienced disease progression, or were unable to complete their therapy due to toxicity/intolerable side effects were considered“incomplete”. A patient who had a “partial” response showed signs of improvement following treatment amounting to at least a 50% reduction in tumor volume.

data.stage:
T stage: T1, T2, T3, T4
N stage: N0, N1, N2, N3
M stage: M0, M1, MX
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
- Patients treated for primary or recurrent SGC between 2002 and 2017 
- All patients provided written informed consent
- Clinical staging via physical exam.
- Imaging (CT/MRI of head and neck; CT of thorax/abdomen or chest X-ray and abdominal ultrasound).
- Patients classified into:
High-risk: ACA, AdCC, SDC, SCC, MEC G3, carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma, oncocytic carcinoma.
Low-risk: ACC, MEC G1/G2, polymorphous adenocarcinoma, epithelial–myoepithelial carcinoma, myoepithelial carcinoma, clear cell carcinoma, basal cell adenocarcinoma.
- SCC Cases included only if no other primary squamous cell carcinoma was detected after thorough staging, including dermatological evaluation.
data.exclusion_criteria:
- Patients with tumors other than salivary gland carcinomas were not included.
- Patients with confirmed primary SCC in another location were excluded to avoid confounding.

endpoint.name: Overall Survival (OS)
endpoint.definition: Time from diagnosis to death from any cause
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Univariate Predictors of Poor Outcome:
Agea (< median vs. ≥ median) p<0.001 
Tumor site (parotid gland vs. other) p= 0.007 Histology (low-risk vs. high-risk) p=0.001  
T-stage (T1–T2 vs. T3–T4) p<0.001 
N-stage (N0 vs. N1–N3) p<0.001
Resection margins (R0 vs. other) p=0.001

Multivariate Predictors of Poor Outcome:
Tumor site (parotid gland vs. other) p= 0.020 
T-stage (T1–T2 vs. T3–T4) p<0.001 
N-stage (N0 vs. N1–N3) p=0.003

data.cancer_type: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
data.other_inclusion_criteria: adult patients with a histopathological or cytological diagnosis of HNSCC involving the lip/oral cavity, oropharynx, hypopharynx or larynx, and initiated first-line systemic therapy for the treatment of locoregional recurrent HNSCC (including second primary tumour and primary tumour relapse) with or without distant metastases, distant metastatic recurrent HNSCC or newly diagnosed metastatic HNSCC (stage IVc), could be included if they participated in a clinical trial at any line of treatment
data.exclusion_criteria: cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma involving the lip; cancers involving the nasopharynx, nasal cavity, paranasal sinuses, salivary glands or thyroid; received locoregional treatment (radiation or surgery) with curative intent in the context of relapsed/metastatic disease; and had not received any systemic therapy for the treatment of R/M HNSCC

data.region:
Europe (Germany, United
Kingdom, Italy, Spain), Asia Pacific (Australia, South Korea, Taiwan), and Latin/North America (Brazil and
Canada)
data.cancer_type: recurrent/metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (R/M HNSCC)
data.other_inclusion_criteria:
Adult patients

Histopathological or cytological diagnosis of HNSCC involving the lip/oral cavity, oropharynx, hypopharynx, or larynx.

Initiated first-line systemic therapy for one of the following:

Locoregional recurrent HNSCC (including second primary tumour and primary tumour relapse),

With or without distant metastases,

Distant metastatic recurrent HNSCC, or

Newly diagnosed metastatic HNSCC (stage IVc).

Patients were eligible even if they participated in a clinical trial at any line of treatment.

endpoint.name: Overall Survival
endpoint.significant_attribute: ECOG performance status

data_source.period:
2012-2013
For sites in Germany, Australia, and Canada: 2011-2013

data.other_inclusion_criteria: primary carcinoma of the parotid gland, and in case of squamous cell carcinoma no hint for primary skin cancer with parotid metastasis.
data.exclusion_criteria:
Patients who were treated for recurrent disease only,
other major or minor salivary gland tumors, lymphoma, skin cancer, metastasis in the parotid region were excluded.
Duplicate records of patients have been removed.

endpoint.name: cancer-specific survival (CSS)
endpoint.significant_attribute: Univariate analysis (significant negative risk factors): Advanced tumor , stage (III/IV), N+, Radiotherapy, Chemotherapy

endpoint.name: overall survival (OS)
endpoint.significant_attribute:
Univariate analysis (significant negative risk factors): Age > 67 years, Advanced tumor stage (III/IV), T3/T4 stage, N+, M+, Grading G3/G4 (poorly differentiated), No surgery, Chemotherapy

Multivariable analysis (independent risk factors depending on model):
-Model including age, T, N, and grading: advanced age > 67 years was the only independent risk factor for OS (p = 0.003).
-Model including T, N, and grading: higher grading (G3/G4) was the only independent risk factor for OS (p = 0.020).
-Model including only T and N: N+ was the independent risk factor for OS (p = 0.021).

data.other_inclusion_criteria:
patients with metastatic HNC had at least one claim with a diagnosis code for HNC, which was identified based on ICD-9-CM codes for
certain malignant neoplasms: 141.xx (malignant neoplasm of tongue), 143.xx (gum), 144.xx (floor of mouth), 145.xx (other and unspecified parts of mouth), 146.xx (oropharynx), 148.xx (hypopharynx) and 161.xx (larynx), free from prior other malignancy, have continuous
health plan eligibility for 6 months before and 6 months after the index date and to be 18 years or older as of the index date, patients had at least two HNC claims that occurred at least 180 days apart which indicates recurrence, received at least one HNC-related treatment (including chemotherapy, targeted therapy or radiation therapy) within a 60-day window around the index date
data.exclusion_criteria: have a claim for metastatic cancer (i.e., secondary tumor ICD-9-CM codes 197.xx and 198.xx) for the period between the initial HNC diagnosis date and 6 months following the index date, could not be matched based on geographic region, gender and age group
